name: Chengdu Taste
tagline: Authentic Sichuan cuisine from the LA-born mini-chain — mouth-numbing ma la flavors that Honolulu's spice lovers have been craving.
mustOrder: boiled fish in chili oil — fiery and numbing
dan dan noodles — rich sesame-chili sauce
talkStory: The LA Sichuan sensation that finally opened in Honolulu, filling a massive gap in the island's Chinese food scene. Authentic Sichuan cooking — real ma la (numbing-spicy) heat from quality peppercorns, not just generic chili oil. The boiled fish in chili oil is a table-wide experience. If you grew up eating Sichuan food or fell in love with it in a mainland Chinatown, this is the real deal in Honolulu. Shares the 808 Center complex with Mian (their noodle concept). Lunch and dinner daily.
